message:
test(frankenterm-core): un-ignore four real-mux snapshot tests now that ft-dvgzi.1.1 is fixed

Removes the `#[ignore = "ft-dvgzi.1.1: ..."]` attribute from four
real-wezterm snapshot tests in `snapshot_real_mux.rs`. These tests
were ignored because the WeztermClient was silently falling back to
the user's interactive mux (`~/.local/share/wezterm/pid`) whenever the
hermetic test socket was briefly unreachable, which made any test
that asserted on pane state see the user's panes instead of the
fixture's panes — an isolation breach.

The underlying bug was just fixed in commit 1e85e8bc3 of this branch:
`WeztermClient::with_socket(...)` now defaults `no_auto_start = true`,
which prepends `--no-auto-start` to every `wezterm cli` invocation
and prevents the autospawn-fallback path from ever firing for an
explicitly-socketed client.

Tests un-ignored:

- `spawn_second_pane_increments_listing` — verifies that spawning a
  second pane via the hermetic CLI bumps the listed pane count from
  1 to 2 against the fixture mux.
- `pane_listing_serializes_and_roundtrips` — verifies that the
  PaneListing returned by the hermetic CLI round-trips through serde
  without losing or mangling fields.
- `restart_fixture_yields_independent_pane_state` — verifies that
  bringing the fixture mux down and back up gives a fresh pane state,
  not a re-attach to the user's persistent mux.
- `wait_until_pane_count_observes_async_spawn` — verifies that the
  async polling path observes the pane count incrementing when a
  pane is spawned in another task. (Polling amplified the original
  bug because each poll cycle re-issued the autostart-fallback.)

These tests still gate on `FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1` so they only
execute when a real wezterm binary + hermetic socket are available.
Default `cargo test` runs continue to skip them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
